This constitutional petition directed against the judgment dated OII2 2OI6 passed by the Addl District Judge, Lahore, whereby the appeal filed by the respondent No 2 was allowed with the result that tne order dated 18.07.2016 allowing eJectment petition filed by the petitioner passed by the Special Judge Rent Lahore was set aside and the matter was remanded Special Judge, Rent decision afresh. Brief facts the C45ا HIL Ihat 11.07.2015, the petitioner rented 0UI Godown measuring 8 Marlas, situated in Yasrab Colony, Baghat Pura, New Shadbagh Lahore t٥ the respondent No2, The petitioner claims In3t tne rent was fixed Rs 3O,DOO ٠ per month and the respondent No.2 amount of Rs1 SO OOO- as security to the petitioner and Ile period of tenancey 145 ] ٠months The paid N0.405٥00[20|6طا respondent No2 sublet the rented premises and 3180 defaulted ٤٨٤ payment of rent, where after tne petitioner 185080 two notices dated 16.10.20I5 16.11.2015, which WI ٨I responded to by Ihe respondent N0.2 and the petitioner filed ejectment petition on 18.12.20I5 The respondent No 2 contested the ejectment petition by filing petition for leave to contest wherein he claimed that rate uf monthly rent ٦٥5 Rs.3000- Unly and ٨UI Rs.30,000/- and Ihe responuent N.2 had paid security aIount Rs.l,5O,UUO- instead of Rs.1,50,000_ the period of tenancy w35 I9-munths ٥٨٥ nDt for | ٠months claimed by the petitioner The Learned Special Judge Rent, Lahore vide order dated 1BUT2UI6 partially dismissed the application for grant of leave to contest un the ground of default and directed the respondent No.2 to hand uver the vacant possession of the demised premises within two months from the date of passing of order As the rate of rent as well as amount deposited 8S security WETE disputed, the petition for leave to COnteSt was allowed that extent and issues were accordingly framed The respondent No 2 filed appeal against Ihe suid order of ejectment 08.09.2016, Which Was cuntesued by the present petitioner The leamed A٥dl District Judge, Lahore, on 01.12.2016 nnd W.P.N%.405IR +L20Ig allowed the appeal 3n٥ 3٤t aside the orUer Uated 18.U72U16 passed by learned Special Judge Rent and remanded the casu to the Special Judge Rent to decide the rent petition after recording of evidence afresh The petitioner has thruugh this constitutional petition challenged the order dated UlI22OI6 passed by the Addl.

This constitutional petition directed against the judgment dated OII2 2OI6 passed by the Addl District Judge, Lahore, whereby the appeal filed by the respondent No 2 was allowed with the result that tne order dated 18.07.2016 allowing eJectment petition filed by the petitioner passed by the Special Judge Rent Lahore was set aside and the matter was remanded Special Judge, Rent decision afresh. Brief facts the C45ا HIL Ihat 11.07.2015, the petitioner rented 0UI Godown measuring 8 Marlas, situated in Yasrab Colony, Baghat Pura, New Shadbagh Lahore t٥ the respondent No2, The petitioner claims In3t tne rent was fixed Rs 3O,DOO ٠ per month and the respondent No.2 amount of Rs1 SO OOO- as security to the petitioner and Ile period of tenancey 145 ] ٠months The paid N0.405٥00[20|6طا respondent No2 sublet the rented premises and 3180 defaulted ٤٨٤ payment of rent, where after tne petitioner 185080 two notices dated 16.10.20I5 16.11.2015, which WI ٨I responded to by Ihe respondent N0.2 and the petitioner filed ejectment petition on 18.12.20I5 The respondent No 2 contested the ejectment petition by filing petition for leave to contest wherein he claimed that rate uf monthly rent ٦٥5 Rs.3000- Unly and ٨UI Rs.30,000/- and Ihe responuent N.2 had paid security aIount Rs.l,5O,UUO- instead of Rs.1,50,000_ the period of tenancy w35 I9-munths ٥٨٥ nDt for | ٠months claimed by the petitioner The Learned Special Judge Rent, Lahore vide order dated 1BUT2UI6 partially dismissed the application for grant of leave to contest un the ground of default and directed the respondent No.2 to hand uver the vacant possession of the demised premises within two months from the date of passing of order As the rate of rent as well as amount deposited 8S security WETE disputed, the petition for leave to COnteSt was allowed that extent and issues were accordingly framed The respondent No 2 filed appeal against Ihe suid order of ejectment 08.09.2016, Which Was cuntesued by the present petitioner The leamed A٥dl District Judge, Lahore, on 01.12.2016 nnd W.P.N%.405IR +L20Ig allowed the appeal 3n٥ 3٤t aside the orUer Uated 18.U72U16 passed by learned Special Judge Rent and remanded the casu to the Special Judge Rent to decide the rent petition after recording of evidence afresh The petitioner has thruugh this constitutional petition challenged the order dated UlI22OI6 passed by the Addl. Distriet Judge Learned counse the petitioner has argued that the respondent No 2 had tempered with the original rent agreement and reduced the rate of rent fronnl Rs.30,000/- Rs.3000/- and enhanced the security amount from Rs,1,50,000/- t0 Rs.11,50,000/- 3n٥ of tenancy from ]-munths Io 1 9-months This fact has been controverted by the counsel for the respondent No2 on the ground that the petitioner had himself produced rent agreement as Mari C wherein Ine rate of rent period of tenancy and amount security were mentioned that supported the version of the respondent No 2 therefore, the order justified MorUver, respondent No had HUvancu amuunt as security, frum which if the rent was Uue, the same could be deducted have gone through the record of the case and the judgments orders passed by the courts below. The learned Special Judge Renit had allowed the period paid N%40500020!6.٣.ا ejectment petition filed by the petitioner on the ground that the respondent Nu had defaulted and had not paid any rent to the petitiuner after 15.10.2015 The respondent No2ء was bound to prove that the rent was paid by him The respondent No 2 denied the fact of being defaulter but could not produce any receipt in suppont of his own version As per the rent agreement, the respondent No. ؟١؟'١٧ bound to pay rent between the 10 and IS" of every month W45 produced by him to show payment of rent, rather the respondent N0.2 filed application under Section 20 Of the Punjab Rented Premises 2U09 for deposit of rent With the Special Judge Rent but the SUI W45 dismissed due to non prosecution on 29 OI2OI6, which gucs on to support to the claim of the petitioner that the rent was not paid by the respondent No The leamed Addl District Judge observed that without recording the evidenee and decision qua the issue, the respondent No 2 cannOt be declared defaulter and if for moment it is believed that that the respondent No 2 failed to deposit the rent as averred by Ine petitioner, sufficient amount of security is deposited with Ihe petitioner and rent can be adjusted from the 83m٤.

The learned Special Judge Renit had allowed the period paid N%40500020!6.٣.ا ejectment petition filed by the petitioner on the ground that the respondent Nu had defaulted and had not paid any rent to the petitiuner after 15.10.2015 The respondent No2ء was bound to prove that the rent was paid by him The respondent No 2 denied the fact of being defaulter but could not produce any receipt in suppont of his own version As per the rent agreement, the respondent No. ؟١؟'١٧ bound to pay rent between the 10 and IS" of every month W45 produced by him to show payment of rent, rather the respondent N0.2 filed application under Section 20 Of the Punjab Rented Premises 2U09 for deposit of rent With the Special Judge Rent but the SUI W45 dismissed due to non prosecution on 29 OI2OI6, which gucs on to support to the claim of the petitioner that the rent was not paid by the respondent No The leamed Addl District Judge observed that without recording the evidenee and decision qua the issue, the respondent No 2 cannOt be declared defaulter and if for moment it is believed that that the respondent No 2 failed to deposit the rent as averred by Ine petitioner, sufficient amount of security is deposited with Ihe petitioner and rent can be adjusted from the 83m٤. The claim of the respondent No2 is that he has deposited security amount as RslSOUUU- and rent uty Nothing Acl, WPNL 4٥5IL%ZUE averred by the respundent No 2 is Rs 3OOU- per which can be deducted from the security amount am not in agreement with the assertions of the respundent No 2 and the reasons given by the Addl. District Judge, whereby it has been observed that outstanding rent could be adjusted against the security amount and the respundent No 2 cannot be declared UelHuIII WIIhOMI recording of evidence because observed by the learned Special Judge Rent in its order that the respunuent No.2 had himself applied 27.1220I5 through an application under Section 20 of the Punjab Rented Premises Act 2009, filed tne court for deposit of rent This in my opinion amounts to admission that the payment of rent W45 due and outstanding on the date of presenting the application The said application was dismissed for non prosecution 29.01.2016 The respondent No neither made any effort to have Ihe application restored, nor tried to pay the rent in the court Or produced any receipt to show that rent has been received by the petitioner, The respondent has not denied in arguments or in grounds of appeal the fact of application f0r deposit of rent in the court The learned Special Judge Rent nas ample jurisdiction regulate ٥٧ procedure un the basis of material available with him month filing علاخfا5ادساطلا Reliance may be placed on cases of Maharmad Taria ططشلا Mششفad aad Asai (2OU7 SCMR 818) and تلفظ فعلا Yaseen (2UII CLC 1464) The learned Special Judge rent procedure of deciding the matter on the basis of facts established on the record need not record evidence for determination ofa established on the record In view of the afore referred circumstances, in mny opinion learned Special Judge Rent was justified to hold the petitioner defaulter and there is WrOng with declining the application for leave to contest to the respondent No 2 to that extent The Addl.

District Judge did not give proper consideration to the afore- referred aspect of the matter and ؟1١٢٩ not justified sel-aside the order of the Special Judge Rent Th observation of Ine Addl. District Judge that the sufficient amount of security has been deposited by the respondent No.2 and rent could be adjusted from that amount is also not based on proper appreciation of facts of the case and law on the subject For this purpose reference may be made to Section 7 the Punjab Rented Premises Act, 2009 reproduced below - ٠ Paymenl UFirent IL or Ienuerع Lلnكس Ihe ففلفdفس in Ine هoفلففعفق in Ihe Ienancy agreemenl فعسعسعه (Emphasis supplied) could ad0٢l a٨d fac nuthing علخفکد عا طا It is clear from the afore referred Section that the tenantrespondent No.2 was obliged to make payment of rent to the petitioner in the mode and by the Uale mentioned ٢٨٤ rent agreement has specifically been mentioned in the rent agreement that the respondent No 2 was obliged to pay rent between IO and 15 of each month. The rent agreement does not provide that the due rent is to be adjusted from the security ١ ٩١٨٨٧٨ The petitioner W45 refund security amount at the time of vacation uf premises by tne respondent No 2, therefore, the availability security amount cannot absolve the respondent No2 to pay rent within The respondent No has failed to show payment of rent to the petitioner in accordance with the tenns of agreement, which the backing of section ofthe Punjab Rented Premises Act, 2009. It is settled law that when law required an act to be done in particular manner it had to be done in that manner alone and such dictate of law cannot be termed as mere technicality Reliance is placed on PLD ZUI6 SC ک99 Shahida B1bi 414 0fner5 ٧٤٢٥٧ #4B!B 84٨ LIITED 4nd 0{/1815) and PLش 203 SC 255 (MUHAMMAD ANWAR and ofher5 VeFSMS Mst ILYAS BEGUVand others tinne has

It is settled law that when law required an act to be done in particular manner it had to be done in that manner alone and such dictate of law cannot be termed as mere technicality Reliance is placed on PLD ZUI6 SC ک99 Shahida B1bi 414 0fner5 ٧٤٢٥٧ #4B!B 84٨ LIITED 4nd 0{/1815) and PLش 203 SC 255 (MUHAMMAD ANWAR and ofher5 VeFSMS Mst ILYAS BEGUVand others tinne has &Ng 40510"20؟ا Besides It is settled proposition uf law that amount of security cannot be adjusted as due rent during the pendency of the relationship uf landlord and tenant between the parties, unless it is expressly otherwise between the parties Reliance in this behalf is placed C3Se ea}؟ A5g4r تة مفسا ممسفا (PLD SC 228) Wherein Ihe Honourable Supreme Court of Pakistan observed under: " Alihough 0/ IenH0ne٥ agreemenl ul I huw und when Ihe security deposii will be udjusied applied, bw eufily b٤ spelled ow Jrom Ihe Ise oFIne word securi ana Ine posifve PrUVIIOnJ FEguFuIng puyment oJ reni advunce Ul Jpectfed also the puymen oJ other churges; und Ihe proviston the negutive Jorm fhur Ihe fenunl Fh٥i IUI CUU5E IO55 dumIUge Io Ine pruperfy Ihع landlord Th& SecurIy depo5fl thercjore, [5 10 be adjusted by the landlurd when the tenuni wucules Ihe premises Iowurds updI FEN Ur ugulnJf ufer DUJenIS OF LOTS 41 0١٤٠»٧١ »١ could nor be udjused Ihe curenl liubilily I puy Ihe rent II hus Io be udjusted in uccordance wiih Ihe Ierms oythe ugreemenl and nor by Ihe Renr Controller @ his will The same principle of law is laid down in the case of Zuففعط ف PLD( مفك طععطلفا I988 SC I90) and Mنzo Aقحه فط Beg فس فا Ahmod Sheikh (9B0 SCMR 834) By following the dictum laid down in the afore referred cases, it is held that the scurity amount cannot be adjusted in the rent amount for the benefit of agreed 1988 Ilne uguinnt 206ا 0لک3س سلا the respundent No 2 for absolving him from his default and declaring him not to be defaulter It is Ueclared that the findings Uf the learned Additional District Judge are not based on correct appreciation of law on the subject and facts of the case As the dispute to the exact amount of security deposit, rate uf rent and duration of tenancy agreement are pending before the learned Special Judge Rent, therefore, this court has not commented upon the saIne.

und Ihe proviston the negutive Jorm fhur Ihe fenunl Fh٥i IUI CUU5E IO55 dumIUge Io Ine pruperfy Ihع landlord Th& SecurIy depo5fl thercjore, [5 10 be adjusted by the landlurd when the tenuni wucules Ihe premises Iowurds updI FEN Ur ugulnJf ufer DUJenIS OF LOTS 41 0١٤٠»٧١ »١ could nor be udjused Ihe curenl liubilily I puy Ihe rent II hus Io be udjusted in uccordance wiih Ihe Ierms oythe ugreemenl and nor by Ihe Renr Controller @ his will The same principle of law is laid down in the case of Zuففعط ف PLD( مفك طععطلفا I988 SC I90) and Mنzo Aقحه فط Beg فس فا Ahmod Sheikh (9B0 SCMR 834) By following the dictum laid down in the afore referred cases, it is held that the scurity amount cannot be adjusted in the rent amount for the benefit of agreed 1988 Ilne uguinnt 206ا 0لک3س سلا the respundent No 2 for absolving him from his default and declaring him not to be defaulter It is Ueclared that the findings Uf the learned Additional District Judge are not based on correct appreciation of law on the subject and facts of the case As the dispute to the exact amount of security deposit, rate uf rent and duration of tenancy agreement are pending before the learned Special Judge Rent, therefore, this court has not commented upon the saIne. However, it is observed that whether duration of tenancy was I or 19 months Ihe same seems to have expired the pendency of the litigation 10. For what has been discussed above, order passed tne Addl. District Judge n0[ sustainable in the eye of law Resultantly, the same is setaside and the order dated 18U7 2UI6 passed by the Special Judge Rent FSLOrd. With the above observation this constitutional petition is allowed Muzamil Akhtar Shabir) Judg telating during
